Scenario.title: APT41 DUST Campaign against Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex The APT41 DUST group is targeting transportation systems using a complex attack chain involving reconnaissance, resource development, and multiple stages of intrusion.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Vulnerability exploit, Malware, Brute-force attack, Credential compromise

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 538 - Name: Open-Source Library Manipulation
stage.description: The APT41 DUST group gathers information about target transportation systems, employees, and infrastructure by collecting publicly available data.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 - Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The group establishes command and control (C&C) servers using compromised or rented infrastructure in the public and private clouds.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36846 [CWE-22] : Unspecified vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook could allow remote code execution
stage.description: The group targets specific employees within the organization with tailored phishing emails containing malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook.

Scenario.summary: The APT41 DUST group employs a complex attack strategy to infiltrate and exfiltrate data from transportation systems, ultimately aiming to cause disruption and gain unauthorized access to sensitive information.
